Successive clan heads, including {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, funded its reconstruction and maintenance.{{Cite report|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o169/documents/sumiyoshijinnjyanosaireikarashinnkyuufunaimatome.pdf |title=Ōita City Historical Landscape Conservation Plan |chapter=Chapter 2: Festivals of Old and New Funai |page=108 |publisher=Ōita City |date=2019-06 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was moved to Minamishinchi (present-day Funaimachi 3-chome) in [[:en:1881|1881]]. However, due to urbanization and space constraints, it was relocated back to its current site in [[:en:1921|1921]]. The former site later became Wakadake Park and was eventually replaced by the Wakadake Building.{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita011a1.pdf |title=Ōita Then and Now: Chapter 11 - Wakamatsu Street |author=Katsumi Watanabe |publisher=Ōita Godō Shimbunsha |date=2007-10-26 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} In [[:en:2003|2003]], part of the shrine's grounds was used for the construction of the Ōita Central Main Road. The shrine buildings were renovated and moved to the western section of the grounds.{{cite news |url=http://www.oitatv.com/chiiki/index.php?id=1078 |title=Passionate Local Spirit: Let's Visit Summer Festivals |newspaper=Ōita Godō Shimbun |date=2009-07-08}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/733 Wakamiya Hachimangū] - Ōita City Tourism Association * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/11467/ Wakamiya Hachimangū Summer Festival] - Tourism Ōita {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (大分市)|version=100315887|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Buildings in Oita, Oita]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Bungo Otomo clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11616256}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11616256}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

revision.text:
[[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11616256]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |名称 = 若宮八幡社 |image = File:Otita wakamiya hachimangu.jpg |所在地 = [[大分県]][[大分市]]上野町10-28 |位置 = |祭神 = 帯中津比古命（[[仲哀天皇]]）
品陀和気命（[[応神天皇]]）
息長帯比賣命（[[神功皇后]]）
大雀命（[[仁徳天皇]]） |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = [[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]） |本殿 = [[八幡造]] |別名 = 若宮様 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月15日]]（夏季大祭） |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Oita|WD=Q199609}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.226887|N|131.614594|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Uenomachi, [[:en:Ōita (city)|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the base of the Ueno Hills in the southern part of Ōita City, facing the Ōita Central Main Road (an urban planned road named Shōnohara-Sano Line) that opened in [[:en:2008|2008]]. It borders the {{ill|Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|ja|大分市立上野ヶ丘中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|1=Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432699}} to the west and the elevated tracks of the Kyūdai Main Line to the north. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in [[:en:1196|1196]] when [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshinao|Ōtomo no Yasunao]], the first head of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]], brought a divided spirit of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] to the region while serving as the governor of the provinces of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] and [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. Initially located in Furukawazuru (present-day Iwata-chō), the shrine was moved to its current eastern site in [[:en:1201|1201]] due to repeated flooding from the Ōita River. The shrine's location is depicted in the "Funaikozu," a map of Funai from the Tenshō period (circa [[:en:1581|1581]] to [[:en:1586|1586]]).{{文化遺産オンライン|95170|府内古図}} Historically, the shrine was revered by the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]], who regarded it as a guardian of their castle in Funai. It was situated in the southwest ({{ill|Kimon|ja|裏鬼門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura Kimon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼門}}, the "back demon gate") relative to the clan's residence. Successive clan heads, including {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, funded its reconstruction and maintenance.{{Cite report|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o169/documents/sumiyoshijinnjyanosaireikarashinnkyuufunaimatome.pdf |title=Ōita City Historical Landscape Conservation Plan |chapter=Chapter 2: Festivals of Old and New Funai |page=108 |publisher=Ōita City |date=2019-06 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was moved to Minamishinchi (present-day Funaimachi 3-chome) in [[:en:1881|1881]]. However, due to urbanization and space constraints, it was relocated back to its current site in [[:en:1921|1921]]. The former site later became Wakadake Park and was eventually replaced by the Wakadake Building.{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita011a1.pdf |title=Ōita Then and Now: Chapter 11 - Wakamatsu Street |author=Katsumi Watanabe |publisher=Ōita Godō Shimbunsha |date=2007-10-26 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} In [[:en:2003|2003]], part of the shrine's grounds was used for the construction of the Ōita Central Main Road. The shrine buildings were renovated and moved to the western section of the grounds.{{cite news |url=http://www.oitatv.com/chiiki/index.php?id=1078 |title=Passionate Local Spirit: Let's Visit Summer Festivals |newspaper=Ōita Godō Shimbun |date=2009-07-08}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/733 Wakamiya Hachimangū] - Ōita City Tourism Association * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/11467/ Wakamiya Hachimangū Summer Festival] - Tourism Ōita {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (大分市)|version=100315887|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Buildings in Oita, Oita]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Bungo Otomo clan]] {{wikidata link|Q11616256}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11616256}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

Initially located in Furukawazuru (present-day Iwata-chō), the shrine was moved to its current eastern site in [[:en:1201|1201]] due to repeated flooding from the Ōita River. The shrine's location is depicted in the "Funaikozu," a map of Funai from the Tenshō period (circa [[:en:1581|1581]] to [[:en:1586|1586]]).{{文化遺産オンライン|95170|府内古図}} Historically, the shrine was revered by the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]], who regarded it as a guardian of their castle in Funai. It was situated in the southwest ({{ill|Kimon|ja|裏鬼門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura Kimon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼門}}, the "back demon gate") relative to the clan's residence. Successive clan heads, including {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, funded its reconstruction and maintenance.{{Cite report|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o169/documents/sumiyoshijinnjyanosaireikarashinnkyuufunaimatome.pdf |title=Ōita City Historical Landscape Conservation Plan |chapter=Chapter 2: Festivals of Old and New Funai |page=108 |publisher=Ōita City |date=2019-06 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was moved to Minamishinchi (present-day Funaimachi 3-chome) in [[:en:1881|1881]]. The former site later became Wakadake Park and was eventually replaced by the Wakadake Building.{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita011a1.pdf |title=Ōita Then and Now: Chapter 11 - Wakamatsu Street |author=Katsumi Watanabe |publisher=Ōita Godō Shimbunsha |date=2007-10-26 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} In [[:en:2003|2003]], part of the shrine's grounds was used for the construction of the Ōita Central Main Road. The shrine buildings were renovated and moved to the western section of the grounds.{{cite news |url=http://www.oitatv.com/chiiki/index.php?id=1078 |title=Passionate Local Spirit: Let's Visit Summer Festivals |newspaper=Ōita Godō Shimbun |date=2009-07-08}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/733 Wakamiya Hachimangū] - Ōita City Tourism Association * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/11467/ Wakamiya Hachimangū Summer Festival] - Tourism Ōita {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (大分市)|version=100315887|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Buildings in Oita, Oita]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Bungo Otomo clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11616256}}

[[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11616256]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |名称 = 若宮八幡社 |image = File:Otita wakamiya hachimangu.jpg |所在地 = [[大分県]][[大分市]]上野町10-28 |位置 = |祭神 = 帯中津比古命（[[仲哀天皇]]）
品陀和気命（[[応神天皇]]）
息長帯比賣命（[[神功皇后]]）
大雀命（[[仁徳天皇]]） |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = [[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]） |本殿 = [[八幡造]] |別名 = 若宮様 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月15日]]（夏季大祭） |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Oita|WD=Q199609}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.226887|N|131.614594|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Uenomachi, [[:en:Ōita (city)|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the base of the Ueno Hills in the southern part of Ōita City, facing the Ōita Central Main Road (an urban planned road named Shōnohara-Sano Line) that opened in [[:en:2008|2008]]. It borders the {{ill|Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|ja|大分市立上野ヶ丘中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|1=Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432699}} to the west and the elevated tracks of the Kyūdai Main Line to the north. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in [[:en:1196|1196]] when [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshinao|Ōtomo no Yasunao]], the first head of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]], brought a divided spirit of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] to the region while serving as the governor of the provinces of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] and [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. Initially located in Furukawazuru (present-day Iwata-chō), the shrine was moved to its current eastern site in [[:en:1201|1201]] due to repeated flooding from the Ōita River. The shrine's location is depicted in the "Funaikozu," a map of Funai from the Tenshō period (circa [[:en:1581|1581]] to [[:en:1586|1586]]).{{文化遺産オンライン|95170|府内古図}} Historically, the shrine was revered by the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]], who regarded it as a guardian of their castle in Funai. It was situated in the southwest ({{ill|Kimon|ja|裏鬼門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura Kimon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼門}}, the "back demon gate") relative to the clan's residence. Successive clan heads, including {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, funded its reconstruction and maintenance.{{Cite report|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o169/documents/sumiyoshijinnjyanosaireikarashinnkyuufunaimatome.pdf |title=Ōita City Historical Landscape Conservation Plan |chapter=Chapter 2: Festivals of Old and New Funai |page=108 |publisher=Ōita City |date=2019-06 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was moved to Minamishinchi (present-day Funaimachi 3-chome) in [[:en:1881|1881]]. However, due to urbanization and space constraints, it was relocated back to its current site in [[:en:1921|1921]]. The former site later became Wakadake Park and was eventually replaced by the Wakadake Building.{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita011a1.pdf |title=Ōita Then and Now: Chapter 11 - Wakamatsu Street |author=Katsumi Watanabe |publisher=Ōita Godō Shimbunsha |date=2007-10-26 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} In [[:en:2003|2003]], part of the shrine's grounds was used for the construction of the Ōita Central Main Road. The shrine buildings were renovated and moved to the western section of the grounds.{{cite news |url=http://www.oitatv.com/chiiki/index.php?id=1078 |title=Passionate Local Spirit: Let's Visit Summer Festivals |newspaper=Ōita Godō Shimbun |date=2009-07-08}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/733 Wakamiya Hachimangū] - Ōita City Tourism Association * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/11467/ Wakamiya Hachimangū Summer Festival] - Tourism Ōita {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (大分市)|version=100315887|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] }} [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Buildings in Oita, Oita]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Bungo Otomo clan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11616256}}

The shrine buildings were renovated and moved to the western section of the grounds.{{cite news |url=http://www.oitatv.com/chiiki/index.php?id=1078 |title=Passionate Local Spirit: Let's Visit Summer Festivals |newspaper=Ōita Godō Shimbun |date=2009-07-08}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/733 Wakamiya Hachimangū] - Ōita City Tourism Association * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/11467/ Wakamiya Hachimangū Summer Festival] - Tourism Ōita {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (大分市)|version=100315887|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11616256}} [[Category:Buildings in Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Bungo Otomo clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616256]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |名称 = 若宮八幡社 |image = File:Otita wakamiya hachimangu.jpg |所在地 = [[大分県]][[大分市]]上野町10-28 |位置 = |祭神 = 帯中津比古命（[[仲哀天皇]]）
品陀和気命（[[応神天皇]]）
息長帯比賣命（[[神功皇后]]）
大雀命（[[仁徳天皇]]） |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = [[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]） |本殿 = [[八幡造]] |別名 = 若宮様 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月15日]]（夏季大祭） |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Oita|WD=Q199609}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.226887|N|131.614594|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Uenomachi, [[:en:Ōita (city)|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the base of the Ueno Hills in the southern part of Ōita City, facing the Ōita Central Main Road (an urban planned road named Shōnohara-Sano Line) that opened in [[:en:2008|2008]]. It borders the {{ill|Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|ja|大分市立上野ヶ丘中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|1=Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432699}} to the west and the elevated tracks of the Kyūdai Main Line to the north. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in [[:en:1196|1196]] when [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshinao|Ōtomo no Yasunao]], the first head of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]], brought a divided spirit of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] to the region while serving as the governor of the provinces of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] and [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. Initially located in Furukawazuru (present-day Iwata-chō), the shrine was moved to its current eastern site in [[:en:1201|1201]] due to repeated flooding from the Ōita River. The shrine's location is depicted in the "Funaikozu," a map of Funai from the Tenshō period (circa [[:en:1581|1581]] to [[:en:1586|1586]]).{{文化遺産オンライン|95170|府内古図}} Historically, the shrine was revered by the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]], who regarded it as a guardian of their castle in Funai. It was situated in the southwest ({{ill|Kimon|ja|裏鬼門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura Kimon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼門}}, the "back demon gate") relative to the clan's residence. Successive clan heads, including {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, funded its reconstruction and maintenance.{{Cite report|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o169/documents/sumiyoshijinnjyanosaireikarashinnkyuufunaimatome.pdf |title=Ōita City Historical Landscape Conservation Plan |chapter=Chapter 2: Festivals of Old and New Funai |page=108 |publisher=Ōita City |date=2019-06 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was moved to Minamishinchi (present-day Funaimachi 3-chome) in [[:en:1881|1881]]. However, due to urbanization and space constraints, it was relocated back to its current site in [[:en:1921|1921]]. The former site later became Wakadake Park and was eventually replaced by the Wakadake Building.{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita011a1.pdf |title=Ōita Then and Now: Chapter 11 - Wakamatsu Street |author=Katsumi Watanabe |publisher=Ōita Godō Shimbunsha |date=2007-10-26 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} In [[:en:2003|2003]], part of the shrine's grounds was used for the construction of the Ōita Central Main Road. The shrine buildings were renovated and moved to the western section of the grounds.{{cite news |url=http://www.oitatv.com/chiiki/index.php?id=1078 |title=Passionate Local Spirit: Let's Visit Summer Festivals |newspaper=Ōita Godō Shimbun |date=2009-07-08}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/733 Wakamiya Hachimangū] - Ōita City Tourism Association * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/11467/ Wakamiya Hachimangū Summer Festival] - Tourism Ōita {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (大分市)|version=100315887|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11616256}} [[Category:Buildings in Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Bungo Otomo clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The former site later became Wakadake Park and was eventually replaced by the Wakadake Building.{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita011a1.pdf |title=Ōita Then and Now: Chapter 11 - Wakamatsu Street |author=Katsumi Watanabe |publisher=Ōita Godō Shimbunsha |date=2007-10-26 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} In [[:en:2003|2003]], part of the shrine's grounds was used for the construction of the Ōita Central Main Road. The shrine buildings were renovated and moved to the western section of the grounds.{{cite news |url=http://www.oitatv.com/chiiki/index.php?id=1078 |title=Passionate Local Spirit: Let's Visit Summer Festivals |newspaper=Ōita Godō Shimbun |date=2009-07-08}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/733 Wakamiya Hachimangū] - Ōita City Tourism Association * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/11467/ Wakamiya Hachimangū Summer Festival] - Tourism Ōita {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (大分市)|version=100315887|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11616256}} [[Category:Buildings in Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Bungo Otomo clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616256]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building |名称 = 若宮八幡社 |image = File:Otita wakamiya hachimangu.jpg |所在地 = [[大分県]][[大分市]]上野町10-28 |位置 = |祭神 = 帯中津比古命（[[仲哀天皇]]）
品陀和気命（[[応神天皇]]）
息長帯比賣命（[[神功皇后]]）
大雀命（[[仁徳天皇]]） |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = [[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]） |本殿 = [[八幡造]] |別名 = 若宮様 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月15日]]（夏季大祭） |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Oita|WD=Q199609}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.226887|N|131.614594|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Uenomachi, [[:en:Ōita (city)|Ōita City]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]]. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the base of the Ueno Hills in the southern part of Ōita City, facing the Ōita Central Main Road (an urban planned road named Shōnohara-Sano Line) that opened in [[:en:2008|2008]]. It borders the {{ill|Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|ja|大分市立上野ヶ丘中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|1=Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432699}} to the west and the elevated tracks of the Kyūdai Main Line to the north. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in [[:en:1196|1196]] when [[:en:Ōtomo Yoshinao|Ōtomo no Yasunao]], the first head of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]], brought a divided spirit of [[:en:Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] to the region while serving as the governor of the provinces of [[:en:Buzen Province|Buzen Province]] and [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]. Initially located in Furukawazuru (present-day Iwata-chō), the shrine was moved to its current eastern site in [[:en:1201|1201]] due to repeated flooding from the Ōita River. The shrine's location is depicted in the "Funaikozu," a map of Funai from the Tenshō period (circa [[:en:1581|1581]] to [[:en:1586|1586]]).{{文化遺産オンライン|95170|府内古図}} Historically, the shrine was revered by the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo clan]], who regarded it as a guardian of their castle in Funai. It was situated in the southwest ({{ill|Kimon|ja|裏鬼門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura Kimon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼門}}, the "back demon gate") relative to the clan's residence. Successive clan heads, including {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, funded its reconstruction and maintenance.{{Cite report|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o169/documents/sumiyoshijinnjyanosaireikarashinnkyuufunaimatome.pdf |title=Ōita City Historical Landscape Conservation Plan |chapter=Chapter 2: Festivals of Old and New Funai |page=108 |publisher=Ōita City |date=2019-06 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} During the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was moved to Minamishinchi (present-day Funaimachi 3-chome) in [[:en:1881|1881]]. However, due to urbanization and space constraints, it was relocated back to its current site in [[:en:1921|1921]]. The former site later became Wakadake Park and was eventually replaced by the Wakadake Building.{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita011a1.pdf |title=Ōita Then and Now: Chapter 11 - Wakamatsu Street |author=Katsumi Watanabe |publisher=Ōita Godō Shimbunsha |date=2007-10-26 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} In [[:en:2003|2003]], part of the shrine's grounds was used for the construction of the Ōita Central Main Road. The shrine buildings were renovated and moved to the western section of the grounds.{{cite news |url=http://www.oitatv.com/chiiki/index.php?id=1078 |title=Passionate Local Spirit: Let's Visit Summer Festivals |newspaper=Ōita Godō Shimbun |date=2009-07-08}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/733 Wakamiya Hachimangū] - Ōita City Tourism Association * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/11467/ Wakamiya Hachimangū Summer Festival] - Tourism Ōita {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (大分市)|version=100315887|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11616256}} [[Category:Buildings in Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Bungo Otomo clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

However, due to urbanization and space constraints, it was relocated back to its current site in {{ill|1921|en|1921|WD=Q2162|lt=1921}}. The former site later became Wakadake Park and was eventually replaced by the Wakadake Building.{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita011a1.pdf |title=Ōita Then and Now: Chapter 11 - Wakamatsu Street |author=Katsumi Watanabe |publisher=Ōita Godō Shimbunsha |date=2007-10-26 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} In {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003}}, part of the shrine's grounds was used for the construction of the Ōita Central Main Road. The shrine buildings were renovated and moved to the western section of the grounds.{{cite news |url=http://www.oitatv.com/chiiki/index.php?id=1078 |title=Passionate Local Spirit: Let's Visit Summer Festivals |newspaper=Ōita Godō Shimbun |date=2009-07-08}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/733 Wakamiya Hachimangū] - Ōita City Tourism Association * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/11467/ Wakamiya Hachimangū Summer Festival] - Tourism Ōita {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (大分市)|version=100315887|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11616256}} [[Category:Buildings in Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Bungo Otomo clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] {{infobox religious building |名称 = 若宮八幡社 |image = File:Otita wakamiya hachimangu.jpg |所在地 = [[大分県]][[大分市]]上野町10-28 |位置 = |祭神 = 帯中津比古命（[[仲哀天皇]]）
品陀和気命（[[応神天皇]]）
息長帯比賣命（[[神功皇后]]）
大雀命（[[仁徳天皇]]） |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = [[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]） |本殿 = [[八幡造]] |別名 = 若宮様 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月15日]]（夏季大祭） |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Oita|WD=Q199609}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.226887|N|131.614594|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455}} }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in Uenomachi, {{ill|Ōita (city)|en|Ōita (city)|WD=Q199609|lt=Ōita City}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the base of the Ueno Hills in the southern part of Ōita City, facing the Ōita Central Main Road (an urban planned road named Shōnohara-Sano Line) that opened in {{ill|2008|en|2008|WD=Q2004|lt=2008}}. It borders the {{ill|Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|ja|大分市立上野ヶ丘中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|1=Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432699}} to the west and the elevated tracks of the Kyūdai Main Line to the north. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in {{ill|1196|en|1196|WD=Q19747|lt=1196}} when {{ill|Ōtomo Yoshinao|en|Ōtomo Yoshinao|WD=Q11433255|lt=Ōtomo no Yasunao}}, the first head of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo clan}}, brought a divided spirit of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} to the region while serving as the governor of the provinces of {{ill|Buzen Province|en|Buzen Province|WD=Q1047134|lt=Buzen Province}} and {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}. Initially located in Furukawazuru (present-day Iwata-chō), the shrine was moved to its current eastern site in {{ill|1201|en|1201|WD=Q5225|lt=1201}} due to repeated flooding from the Ōita River. The shrine's location is depicted in the "Funaikozu," a map of Funai from the Tenshō period (circa {{ill|1581|en|1581|WD=Q6717|lt=1581}} to {{ill|1586|en|1586|WD=Q6739|lt=1586}}).{{文化遺産オンライン|95170|府内古図}} Historically, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo clan}}, who regarded it as a guardian of their castle in Funai. It was situated in the southwest ({{ill|Kimon|ja|裏鬼門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura Kimon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼門}}, the "back demon gate") relative to the clan's residence. Successive clan heads, including {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, funded its reconstruction and maintenance.{{Cite report|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o169/documents/sumiyoshijinnjyanosaireikarashinnkyuufunaimatome.pdf |title=Ōita City Historical Landscape Conservation Plan |chapter=Chapter 2: Festivals of Old and New Funai |page=108 |publisher=Ōita City |date=2019-06 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the shrine was moved to Minamishinchi (present-day Funaimachi 3-chome) in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881}}. However, due to urbanization and space constraints, it was relocated back to its current site in {{ill|1921|en|1921|WD=Q2162|lt=1921}}. The former site later became Wakadake Park and was eventually replaced by the Wakadake Building.{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita011a1.pdf |title=Ōita Then and Now: Chapter 11 - Wakamatsu Street |author=Katsumi Watanabe |publisher=Ōita Godō Shimbunsha |date=2007-10-26 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} In {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003}}, part of the shrine's grounds was used for the construction of the Ōita Central Main Road. The shrine buildings were renovated and moved to the western section of the grounds.{{cite news |url=http://www.oitatv.com/chiiki/index.php?id=1078 |title=Passionate Local Spirit: Let's Visit Summer Festivals |newspaper=Ōita Godō Shimbun |date=2009-07-08}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/733 Wakamiya Hachimangū] - Ōita City Tourism Association * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/11467/ Wakamiya Hachimangū Summer Festival] - Tourism Ōita {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (大分市)|version=100315887|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11616256}} [[Category:Buildings in Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Bungo Otomo clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

息長帯比賣命（[[神功皇后]]）
大雀命（[[仁徳天皇]]） |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = [[建久]]7年（[[1196年]]） |本殿 = [[八幡造]] |別名 = 若宮様 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[7月15日]]（夏季大祭） |神事 = |prefecture = {{ill|Oita|WD=Q199609|1=Ōita (city)}} |coordinates = {{Coord|33.226887|N|131.614594|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Hachiman-zukuri|WD=Q3125455|1=Hachiman-zukuri}} }} '''Wakamiya Hachimangū''' (若宮八幡社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Uenomachi, {{ill|Ōita (city)|en|Ōita (city)|WD=Q199609|lt=Ōita City|1=Ōita (city)}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}. == Overview == The shrine is situated at the base of the Ueno Hills in the southern part of Ōita City, facing the Ōita Central Main Road (an urban planned road named Shōnohara-Sano Line) that opened in {{ill|2008|en|2008|WD=Q2004|lt=2008|1=2008}}. It borders the {{ill|Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|ja|大分市立上野ヶ丘中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōita Municipal Uenogaoka Junior High School|1=Q11432699|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11432699}} to the west and the elevated tracks of the Kyūdai Main Line to the north. == History == According to the shrine's tradition, it was established in {{ill|1196|en|1196|WD=Q19747|lt=1196|1=1196}} when {{ill|Ōtomo Yoshinao|en|Ōtomo Yoshinao|WD=Q11433255|lt=Ōtomo no Yasunao|1=Ōtomo Yoshinao}}, the first head of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan}}, brought a divided spirit of {{ill|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|en|Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|WD=Q701403|lt=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū|1=Tsurugaoka Hachimangū}} to the region while serving as the governor of the provinces of {{ill|Buzen Province|en|Buzen Province|WD=Q1047134|lt=Buzen Province|1=Buzen Province}} and {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}. Initially located in Furukawazuru (present-day Iwata-chō), the shrine was moved to its current eastern site in {{ill|1201|en|1201|WD=Q5225|lt=1201|1=1201}} due to repeated flooding from the Ōita River. The shrine's location is depicted in the "Funaikozu," a map of Funai from the Tenshō period (circa {{ill|1581|en|1581|WD=Q6717|lt=1581|1=1581}} to {{ill|1586|en|1586|WD=Q6739|lt=1586|1=1586}}).{{文化遺産オンライン|95170|府内古図}} Historically, the shrine was revered by the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan}}, who regarded it as a guardian of their castle in Funai. It was situated in the southwest ({{ill|Kimon|ja|裏鬼門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ura Kimon|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鬼門}}, the "back demon gate") relative to the clan's residence. Successive clan heads, including {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, funded its reconstruction and maintenance.{{Cite report|和書 |url=https://www.city.oita.oita.jp/o169/documents/sumiyoshijinnjyanosaireikarashinnkyuufunaimatome.pdf |title=Ōita City Historical Landscape Conservation Plan |chapter=Chapter 2: Festivals of Old and New Funai |page=108 |publisher=Ōita City |date=2019-06 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} During the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was moved to Minamishinchi (present-day Funaimachi 3-chome) in {{ill|1881|en|1881|WD=Q7808|lt=1881|1=1881}}. However, due to urbanization and space constraints, it was relocated back to its current site in {{ill|1921|en|1921|WD=Q2162|lt=1921|1=1921}}. The former site later became Wakadake Park and was eventually replaced by the Wakadake Building.{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita011a1.pdf |title=Ōita Then and Now: Chapter 11 - Wakamatsu Street |author=Katsumi Watanabe |publisher=Ōita Godō Shimbunsha |date=2007-10-26 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} In {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003|1=2003}}, part of the shrine's grounds was used for the construction of the Ōita Central Main Road. The shrine buildings were renovated and moved to the western section of the grounds.{{cite news |url=http://www.oitatv.com/chiiki/index.php?id=1078 |title=Passionate Local Spirit: Let's Visit Summer Festivals |newspaper=Ōita Godō Shimbun |date=2009-07-08}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/733 Wakamiya Hachimangū] - Ōita City Tourism Association * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/11467/ Wakamiya Hachimangū Summer Festival] - Tourism Ōita {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (大分市)|version=100315887|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:若宮八幡社 (大分市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11616256}} [[Category:Buildings in Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Bungo Otomo clan]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

However, due to urbanization and space constraints, it was relocated back to its current site in {{ill|1921|en|1921|WD=Q2162|lt=1921|1=1921}}. The former site later became Wakadake Park and was eventually replaced by the Wakadake Building.{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.nan-nan.jp/lib/oita011a1.pdf |title=Ōita Then and Now: Chapter 11 - Wakamatsu Street |author=Katsumi Watanabe |publisher=Ōita Godō Shimbunsha |date=2007-10-26 |accessdate=2020-01-13}} In {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003|1=2003}}, part of the shrine's grounds was used for the construction of the Ōita Central Main Road. The shrine buildings were renovated and moved to the western section of the grounds.{{cite news |url=http://www.oitatv.com/chiiki/index.php?id=1078 |title=Passionate Local Spirit: Let's Visit Summer Festivals |newspaper=Ōita Godō Shimbun |date=2009-07-08}} == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [https://www.oishiimati-oita.jp/spots/detail/733 Wakamiya Hachimangū] - Ōita City Tourism Association * [https://www.visit-oita.jp/events/detail/11467/ Wakamiya Hachimangū Summer Festival] - Tourism Ōita {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|若宮八幡社 (大分市)|version=100315887|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11616256|ja|若宮八幡社 (大分市)}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Bungo Otomo clan]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Oita, Oita]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines dedicated to Empress Jingū]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
